summary: Iteration 3 VideoPlayer overlay fully verified. Backend pytest 28/28 still green (no regression). Frontend Playwright 43/45 effective passes covering all review-request criteria: in-app overlay opens on card click (old MediaDetailsDialog gone), direct MP4 plays via <video> with autoplay + controlsList contains 'nodownload', YouTube embed iframe src starts with https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/jfKfPfyJRdk, Vimeo iframe src starts with https://player.vimeo.com/video/76979871 with dnt=1/title=0/byline=0/portrait=0, iframe sandbox = 'allow-scripts allow-same-origin allow-presentation' (NO allow-popups, NO allow-top-navigation — verified via getAttribute), zero <a> tags in overlay, no 'watch on'/'open original' text, both click-shields rendered, source badge + title + description footer correct (description absent when missing), Escape key closes, body overflow locked to hidden while open and restored on close, ProfileShell restored after close, Edit button closes player + opens MediaForm prefilled (save updates the card), Delete button opens confirm dialog and confirming removes the card from grid + closes overlay, section filter pills + Add-media button still functional (no regression). The 2 'failures' in the script log are not bugs — the source-badge text-transform:uppercase CSS makes inner_text return 'DIRECT'/'YOUTUBE'; the underlying label literals in code are correctly 'Direct'/'YouTube'.
critical_code_review_comments: iframe attribute warning in browser console: "Allow attribute will take precedence over 'allowfullscreen'." — harmless (since allow already lists fullscreen) but you can drop the redundant allowFullScreen prop in VideoPlayer.jsx to silence the warning.
Carry-over: server.py still uses deprecated @app.on_event('shutdown'); migrate to FastAPI lifespan handlers.
Carry-over: consider Mongo index on media.profileId once item counts grow.

Right-click suppression on the overlay works at the React-handler level; for a hardened defense you could also add onMouseDown right-button trap, but current behaviour blocks the browser context menu as required.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (45/45 real checks; 2 log 'fails' are test-string case mismatches caused by CSS uppercase transform, code labels are correct)

test_credentials: Master admin passcode: 1115 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Per-test profile passcodes are arbitrary 4-digit (4242/5678/9999).
seed_data_creation: Playwright run created TEST_PWPlayer profile with 3 media items (TEST_DirectMP4, TEST_YT YouTube, TEST_Vimeo). Cleaned up via DELETE /api/admin/profiles/{id} in finally block (cascade removes media).
context_for_next_testing_agent: VideoPlayer test ids in use: video-player-overlay, player-source-badge (uppercase via CSS — read textContent not inner_text if asserting exact case), player-title, player-edit-btn, player-delete-btn, player-close-btn, player-iframe, player-video, player-shield-top, player-shield-bottom-right, player-description, player-delete-confirm-dialog, player-delete-cancel, player-delete-confirm. Iframe sandbox is exactly 'allow-scripts allow-same-origin allow-presentation'. parseSource normalises YouTube to youtube-nocookie.com/embed/{id} and Vimeo to player.vimeo.com/video/{id} with strict params. Body overflow restoration uses prevOverflow snapshot, so after close document.body.style.overflow returns to its prior empty string (not 'auto'). MediaCard onClick passes the entire media object up to setPlaying; the OLD MediaDetailsDialog file no longer exists (do not reference media-details-* testIds). Backend is untouched in this iter.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found in iteration 3.
